name: kr8s (Ergonomic Python Kubernetes Client)

auth.notes: Automatic kubeconfig or in-cluster service account detection

performance.notes: httpx-based async; same API server round-trip as kubectl

agent_readiness.notes: Modern ergonomic Kubernetes client with sync and async APIs. import kr8s. pods = kr8s.get('pods', namespace='default'). pod = kr8s.get('pod/my-pod', namespace='default'). pod.delete(). Async: await kr8s.asyncio.get('pods'). Object-oriented: pod.restart(), pod.logs(). Automatic kubeconfig detection. Scale: deployment.scale(5). Port-forward context manager. Patch: pod.patch({'metadata': {'labels': {'key': 'val'}}}). Much simpler than official kubernetes-client.

gotchas: Newer library — less battle-tested than official kubernetes-client
CRD support via custom resource class definitions
Some advanced operations still require falling back to kubernetes-client
API coverage may lag behind official client for newest K8s features
best_when: Python automation scripts that interact with Kubernetes
When official kubernetes-client is too verbose
Pod management, deployment scaling, log tailing in Python
avoid_when: Complex operator patterns (kopf has better lifecycle hooks)
When official kubernetes-client compatibility is required
Production operators with complex CRD management
summary: kr8s is an ergonomic Python Kubernetes client. kr8s.get('pods', namespace='default'); pod.delete(), pod.scale(5), pod.logs(). Sync and async APIs (await kr8s.asyncio.get('pods')). Object-oriented resource access. Automatic kubeconfig detection. Much simpler than official kubernetes-client. Use for automation scripts and light operator work. Use kopf for production operator patterns.
